compiled_state.author: Iso Tester
compiled_state.description:
## Why
The plan-review gate shipped advisory-by-default with uniform thresholds
(block_threshold=0.95, default_posture=advisory for all 32 criteria) so it could
not block during calibration. We have now dogfooded enough to calibrate per-criterion
defaults from real data: 167 REVIEW_RESULT sidecars / 106 tickets / 1,329 findings.

## What
Derive per-criterion (default_posture, block_threshold) defaults from the dogfooded
REVIEW_RESULT corpus, using two reliability signals:
- Verifier-refutation (dense form): per-criterion `validity` distribution, P(dropped),
  P(indeterminate), and the per-binary-subquestion "no" profile. (The literal `dropped`
  outcome alone is too sparse — 18 total — so use continuous validity.)
- Voluntary revision-response: a material_fingerprint change between consecutive reviews
  of a ticket = a genuine revision. Exact finding-id survival is CONFOUNDED by finder
  text-churn (~100% false "resolution" for LLM criteria vs 13% for the one deterministic
  criterion P6), so attribute revisions at criterion-load-delta granularity, not exact id.
- Fire-rate denominator from coverage.routing (which criteria ran per review).

## Scope
1. Sidecar enrichment (APPROVED, must stay UX-invisible to library/MCP/CLI): persist
   fields that make the revision signal cleanly measurable going forward (normalized
   finding id + location), without changing the surfaced verdict shape.
2. Offline calibration analysis over the sidecars -> per-criterion reliability profile +
   proposed (posture, threshold).
3. Step 5 = INTERACTIVE review with the user for borderline / low-sample criteria
   (block vs advisory) — some may be clear blockers despite small n.
4. Apply agreed thresholds to src/rebar/llm/plan_review/criteria_routing.json
   (block_threshold + default_posture per criterion; registry reads it directly).
5. Step 7 validation: run plan-review with the new thresholds on 20 randomly-sampled
   tickets that historically had high finding counts or overlay triggers. If any BLOCK,
   discuss with the user; otherwise the plan is approved.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Sidecar enrichment lands with a test proving the surfaced verdict (library/MCP/CLI
      return shapes) is byte-unchanged — enrichment is observability-only.
- [ ] A reproducible offline calibration script reads the sidecars and emits a
      per-criterion table: n, mean validity, P(dropped), P(indeterminate), revision-response
      (criterion-load-delta), fire-rate, and a proposed (posture, threshold).
- [ ] Step 5 interactive decision is recorded (which criteria flip to blocking and at what
      threshold, which stay advisory, and the rationale per criterion).
- [ ] criteria_routing.json updated to the agreed per-criterion defaults; lint + the
      plan-review unit tests pass.
- [ ] Step 7: 20 sampled tickets re-reviewed under the new thresholds; the block outcomes
      are reported. Zero unexpected blocks => approved; any block => discussed.
compiled_state.title: Calibrate plan-review per-criterion block thresholds from dogfooded REVIEW_RESULT data
